title: Remobilization Services | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Remobilization Services

### Remobilize quickly in a post-pandemic world.
The COVID-19 pandemic has had a major impact on global mobility programs worldwide.
From the introduction of immigration policies to protect resident labor, to the shift to remote work, to the additional burden of health-related restrictions on short-term travel—trends and legislation continue to fluctuate.
As entry bans are lifted and companies feel the pressure to remobilize quickly, the global rebound in business travel will be unprecedented. Travelers and employers will need to comply with multiple sets of unclear and ever-changing rules and requirements—both going to and coming from across various countries, with serious consequences for noncompliance.
Fragomen provides strategic support to help your company prepare for the potential impacts to their programs and navigate an instable environment so that you can get your mobile workforce back on the ground.

### The latest on COVID-19 restrictions in real-time.
We have a dedicated team monitoring the latest changes on immigration restrictions and concessions.
Our Immigration Update: Coronavirus matrix captures up-to-date information on more than 150 international jurisdictions, including entry or transit bans and mandatory quarantines. This real-time information is frequently updated throughout each day.

### Staying ahead of change.
The global recession caused by COVID-19 containment measures has driven unemployment rates and shifted workplace practices. In response, policymakers are focused on protecting resident labor and authorities have increased attention to compliance.
Fragomen is at the forefront of monitoring these changes. We are preparing our clients for a post-COVID environment through:
- Audits and remediation of internal processes Assessment reviews Change management and recruitment strategies Government strategies advice and holistic compliance support
- Audits and remediation of internal processes Assessment reviews Change management and recruitment strategies Government strategies advice and holistic compliance support
- Audits and remediation of internal processes
- Change management and recruitment strategies
- Government strategies advice and holistic compliance support

### Agile solutions for the future of work.
The pivot to remote work since the pandemic has further complicated the mobility landscape, presenting a gap between the new culture of work and the existing regulatory framework in many countries.
We have been partnering with our clients to create agile solutions that maintain compliance in this unprecedented time, including guidance on:
- Supporting staff to work from remote locations Virtual internships Starting graduate schemes from remote locations Ensuring compliance on special working arrangements Liaising with other global mobility vendors for a holistic view of global mobility issues
- Supporting staff to work from remote locations Virtual internships Starting graduate schemes from remote locations Ensuring compliance on special working arrangements Liaising with other global mobility vendors for a holistic view of global mobility issues
- Supporting staff to work from remote locations
- Starting graduate schemes from remote locations
- Ensuring compliance on special working arrangements
- Liaising with other global mobility vendors for a holistic view of global mobility issues
As short-term travel rapidly increases, our Nomadic tool provides compliance and technology support across borders. And our Government Strategies and Compliance Group can help you craft targeted and innovative strategies for success.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
